MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-09-2016 Vacated

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-31-2016 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-11-2016 Continued To Nov-14-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Paige Yeh of Goodman Neuman Hamilton LLP telephone number 415-705-0160 for
Cross-Complainant/Cross-Defendant Lee's Imperial Welding, Inc.
Ryan Palumbo of Collins Collins Muir + Stewart LLP telephone number 510-844-5100 for Defendant Mark Cavagnero Associates.
James Olson of Christensen Ehret LLP telephone 916-433-6909 appeared by telephone for Cross-Defendant Hathaway Dinwiddie Construction.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
In re: CROSS-DEFENDANT MARK CAVAGNERO ASSOCIATES' DEMURRER TO CROSS-COMPLAINT - MCA's request for judicial notice, which is unopposed, is GRANTED. MCA's demurrer is OVERRULED. MCA has failed to establish that Lee's cross-claims against it are barred by the completed and accepted doctrine because the allegations of the cross-complaint and the evidence properly the subject of judicial review do not demonstrate that Lee's cross-claims are barred by the completed and accepted doctrine. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318 [demurrer may challenge the legal sufficiency of a pleading based on the face of the pleadings and matters that are judicially noticeable]; Cantu v. Resolution Trust Corp. (1992) 4 Cal.App.4th 857, 877 [judicially noticeable facts not set forth in the complaint may be considered on demurrer].) On this record, the court cannot find as a matter of law that the project had been completed and accepted by owner SF Jazz prior to January 21, 2013, the date of the incident at issue in the complaint. (See Compl. 7.) Nor can the court find as a matter of law that the defect was patent. (See Compl. 8.) In light of these findings, the court need not address the vitality of the completed and accepted doctrine. MCA is granted 14-days leave to answer. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
